changes_made: Changed k8s_service_mesh_traffic_encryption_enabled to k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled for clarity and specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to information flow enforcement. Network policies and TLS for ingress are appropriate measures for logical separation. The use of a service mesh for traffic encryption is valid, but the naming can be improved for clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'mtls' instead of 'traffic_encryption' to specify mutual TLS in service mesh contexts
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | physical or logical separation of information flo
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(21) focuses on enforcing information flow through physical or logical separation. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved by using NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that only intended communication paths are allowed. Additionally, enabling TLS for Ingress and using a service mesh for traffic encryption further enforces logical separation and secure information flow.
